title: Dense Smoked Ukrainian Bacon Borscht
description: A rich and savory Ukrainian dish featuring smoked streaky bacon, steaming hot beets, and a hint of Ayurvedic flair.

ingredients.name: Smoked streaky bacon
ingredients.notes: Use a thick-cut Ukrainian-style smoked bacon for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Beets
ingredients.notes: Choose a combination of red and golden beets for a stunning color.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Use a sweet onion like Vidalia or Maui for added depth.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic and saut9 in a little bit of oil to prevent burning.

ingredients.name: Cabbage
ingredients.notes: Shred the cabbage finely for a tender texture.

ingredients.name: Carrots
ingredients.notes: Thinly slice the carrots for a pop of color.

ingredients.name: Pickled dill
ingredients.notes: Use a store-bought or homemade pickled dill for added tanginess.

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: Freshly grind the pepper for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Tomato paste
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality tomato paste for a rich flavor.

ingredients.name: Vegan sour cream
ingredients.notes: Choose a plant-based sour cream alternative for a sattvic twist.

instructions: Line a terracotta dish with foil for easy cleanup.
Arrange the beets in the dish, leaving some space between each beet.
Drizzle the beets with a little bit of oil and sprinkle with salt.
Roast the beets in the oven for 30 minutes, or until tender.
While the beets are roasting, cook the bacon in a pan over medium heat until crispy.
Remove the bacon from the pan and set aside.
In the same pan, saut9 the onion and garlic until softened.
Add the cabbage, carrots, and pickled dill to the pan and cook until the vegetables are tender.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the roasted beets to the pan and stir to combine.
Season the borscht with black pepper and vegan sour cream to taste.
Serve the borscht hot in the terracotta dish.
Garnish with crispy bacon and fresh dill, if desired.
